regions.name: Verdant Hollow
regions.description: The temperate cradle where the last easy living gets done. Its Gestarium sleeps lightly.

regions.name: Mirrormere
regions.description: Lakes clear enough to see what the old world dumped in them.

regions.name: Stormcap Heights
regions.description: Mountains that keep their own weather and their own grudges.

regions.name: Ashvein Ridge
regions.description: A caldera country still deciding whether it is done erupting.

regions.name: Sporefen
regions.description: A swamp that digests what it is given. It has been given a great deal.

regions.name: Bonewhite Flats
regions.description: The first region the old war killed. Nothing here has forgiven anyone.

lines: You hear it too, don't you. Engines in the green you gave us back.
Someone found the Cradle warm and decided the old way was owed another try. Go to the heart. End what you started — or finish it properly this time.
The birthing halls are quiet. First morning in forty years nobody rode out at dawn.
Whatever you chose down there — the Hollow owes you. This is everything the watch could spare.
Safe. That's the word everyone keeps using. The meadow's gone gray at the edges, you noticed?
Graveyard peace is still peace. I'll take it. I'll spend the rest of my life telling myself I'd take it again.
Green's coming back like the land was only holding its breath. Children swim in the mere now.
And every trader that passes through looks at that warm, working factory a little too long. Fund the watch, rider. Prosperity forgets fastest.
They say you grow your own now, down in the wombs. That the vats answer to your hand.
The Progenitors probably had a first one too. Someone decent, who only meant to protect everyone. Don't make me right about you.
Fernhollow holds because somebody rides out every morning and thins what the Cradle births.
Lately that somebody is you. No family waiting on you — makes you brave. Or spendable. Same thing here.
The bench in town will graft anything you can carry home. Mind the strain. The little ones break quiet.
The meadow went quiet. Mama says quiet is good.
I haven't seen a Volpi in ages. Did the quiet take them too?
There are flowers on the old road! Actual flowers! I counted forty!
Mama says I might not need a gun-arm after all. Don't tell her I cried.
I saw a Volpi once! It let me touch its nose!
Mama says when I'm big I get grafted a gun-arm like everyone. I don't want one. Don't tell.
Get off the grass. I mean it. The things moving through it now are armed, and they aren't factory-born — someone MADE them.
We revived this place. Us. Which means whoever's grafting out there learned it was possible from watching us.
Grass is thicker than my whole life I've seen it. New burrows every morning.
Almost makes you forget what the ground under it can build. Almost.
Scouting's easy now. Nothing moves. You can hear your own heart out here.
I used to complain about the noise. Turns out the noise was the world.
Grass moves wrong out here sometimes. That's how you tell the wild ones from the factory-born.
Wild ones just want to be left alone. Subdue them gentle — crack the shell, never the thing inside.

npcs.name: Toll-Keeper Vex
lines: No toll. Haven't collected one in thirty years. I just like knowing who's still moving.
North-west takes you to Mirrormere's lakes. North-east climbs to Stormcap. Dead east is Ashvein, if the ground will have you.
South roads? Sporefen to the east of them, and the Bonewhite Flats west. Nobody comes back from the Flats complaining, because nobody comes back talkative.
Six factories, six roads, one of you. Somebody did the arithmetic wrong, and it wasn't me.
Isa sent you sniffing at the Cradle, didn't she. Of course she did. Orphans are cheap.
Listen. I pulled this spitter off a war-unit that nearly took my leg. If you're going in those doors, it should go with you. Better on your beast than in my shed.
So the wombs turn for you now. Good. Or terrible. My trade doesn't get to be picky about which.
Whatever you grow down there — name them. Things with names are harder to spend.
You actually walked out of there. Front doors, even.
The forest sounds different with the heart quiet. Not better. Not worse. Different. I keep listening for it anyway.
That's the Cradle behind me. Doors sealed since before my mother's mother. Pray they stay that way.
Everything it births comes out already armed. Imagine being born with the gun grown on.
When the doors open — and they will — you'll want more than two light mounts. Come find me first.
